$comment: D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-INIT (FC8) end-to-end: aggregate INITIALIZATION of a bit-field struct (`struct S s = {…};`) packs each bit-field into its allocation unit — the path that previously fail-louded (a plain field-wise init writes each field full-width, clobbering co-resident neighbours). Covers a GLOBAL init (static-data byte encoder packs at compile time) AND a LOCAL init (per-unit slot packing at run time), POSITIONAL and DESIGNATED, with an anonymous zero-width bit-field a positional init must SKIP (C 6.7.9), adjacent packing (a:3+b:5 in one unit), a signed sign-extending field (s:4=-3), and neighbour preservation inside a unit. ALSO covers F1 (review-caught): an ordinary field (`char x`) declared BEFORE a bit-field whose allocation unit overlaps it (`struct T`), as BOTH a global (`gt`) and a local (`lt`) — the unit zero-fill must not clobber x, and the two paths must agree (a lazy per-unit zero in declaration order wiped the LOCAL x → exit 35, while the global stayed correct; the MIR two-pass fix zeroes every unit before any field write). Four S structs each net 55 (a=5,b=20,s=-3,c=33); two T structs each net 21 (x=7,a=5,b=9) → tv=42. 4*55 + (ld.pad-lp.pad=0) + 42 - 220 = 42. LAYOUT-RULE-AGNOSTIC: write+read share the gnu_packed rule, so the exit is correct on every target regardless of ABI-exactness (D-CSUBSET-BITFIELD-ABI-EXACT). A wrong pack, missing anon-skip, mis-extracted field, missing sign-extension, clobbered neighbour, or clobbered ordinary-field-in-unit flips the exit. The optimizedPipelines arm proves the flow survives Mem2Reg/ConstFold (the baseline arm is the real-codegen memory witness). arm64 runs under qemu; macho on the macos-latest leg.
